title: G. G.
content:
Each one, when seen by himself, is passably wise and judicious;
    When they in corpore are, naught but a blockhead is seen.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "G. G.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
